construct_verdict_reason: Most items validly measure scientific and educational cultural capital through books and digital infrastructure, but one item measures educational resources that are functional/curricular rather than cultural capital.

items.reason: Directly measures presence of scientific books, a core component of scientific cultural capital.
Directly measures presence of technical books, a core component of scientific and technical cultural capital.
Directly measures presence of encyclopedic books, explicitly mentioned in the construct description.
Directly measures presence of history books, explicitly mentioned in the construct description.
School textbooks measure functional educational resources for academic performance rather than cultural capital reflecting exposure to scientific and educational culture.
Directly measures access to computers, a core digital infrastructure component of the construct.
Directly measures access to internet, a core digital infrastructure component of the construct.
